plainEnglish: If audio plays automatically for more than 3 seconds, give users a way to pause, stop, or mute it independently of their system volume. Auto-playing sound interferes with screen readers (which are also speech) and distracts everyone. The safest practice is not to autoplay audio at all.
whyItMatters: Auto-playing audio drowns out a screen reader's speech, making the whole page unusable for blind users until they can stop it.
quickCheck: Load the page and listen: if anything plays for over 3 seconds, confirm there's a visible pause/stop/mute control; axe's no-autoplay-audio rule flags autoplaying media.
commonMistakes: Adding autoplay to a <video> or <audio> with background sound and no stop or mute control.
Relying on the browser to mute autoplay, which isn't guaranteed across browsers.
Hiding or delaying the pause control so users can't stop the audio quickly.

codeExamples.label: Autoplaying looping video with sound and no control
